title: Causal Observability, Freshness SLOs, Capacity, and Cost · Spiral Systems Review
summary: A delayed operations review reconstructs causal observability, freshness slos, capacity, and cost from revision, tenant, source URL, request validators, response bytes, parsed identity, transaction, lease, state transition, output, failure, cleanup, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for privacy-minimized feed-service operations report after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, an…
objectives: Correlate request, tenant-safe source key, schedule claim, fetch attempt, validator, parse, transaction, and response without leaking raw private identifiers.
Emit bounded structured events with causal error identity and tested redaction for URLs, content, queries, headers, tokens, SQL, and personal state.
Define availability, successful-poll, publisher-respect, ingestion-lag, reader-latency, and restore indicators with windows and error budgets.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Feed Go Correlation Model
steps.instruction:
Order the Feed Go Correlation Model Feed aggregation and Go source-to-reader-and-recovery evidence cycle for this review task before implementing it.

content.text: A review Go team handles case 7318 during an accessible community-news reader review. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove privacy-minimized causal telemetry, freshness SLOs, bounded cardinality, and representative capacity; every goroutine, body, row set, transaction, lease, timer, and pool needs named cleanup ownership; then change one RSS, Atom, namespace, date, identity, content, or encoding case and reconcile provenance. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Correlate request, tenant-safe source key, schedule claim, fetch attempt, validator, parse, transaction, and response without leaking raw private identifiers.

lines.text: A handler requests-per-second benchmark proves ingestion capacity.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Load representative publishers, item sizes, update rates, tenants, reads, searches, failures, and restores against CPU, memory, connections, locks, and budgets. Artifact under review: privacy-minimized feed-service operations report.

options.text: For separate evidence from confidence, import one convenient trusted RSS feed, omit reader and publisher outcomes, revision, discovery and SSRF scope, Atom and namespace cases, XML and HTTP budgets, PostgreSQL constraints and transaction, schedule, lease, goroutine cleanup, tenant, accessibility, recovery, and controlled native transfer, then accept plausible rows as feed-service proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Feed Go Capacity Model, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim does not survive the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Record what required effort · Review Feed Go Capacity Model
steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Feed Go Capacity Model: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A review Go team handles case 5076 during a publisher-fairness incident rehearsal. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove privacy-minimized causal telemetry, freshness SLOs, bounded cardinality, and representative capacity; failed, canceled, stale, and cross-tenant operations must preserve the prior admitted state; then change one artifact, deployment, incident, backup, restore, rollback, or capacity condition and reconcile the release. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Load representative publishers, item sizes, update rates, tenants, reads, searches, failures, and restores against CPU, memory, connections, locks, and budgets.

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and Go Record what required effort · Review Feed Go Capacity Model.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Feed aggregation and Go Record what required effort · Review Feed Go Capacity Model, keep Feed Go Capacity Model connected to executable Go feed, XML, HTTP, PostgreSQL, schedule, API, reader, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Load representative publishers, item sizes, update rates, tenants, reads, searches, failures, and restores against CPU, memory, connections, locks, and budgets.

Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.
